verdict_reason: TRPCClientError still fires; cause is NOT envelope shape (array vs object) but a JSON.parse SyntaxError on the response body. V5-B does not address the real bug.

network_observations.response_body_capture: Failed to load response data — DevTools could not retrieve body (likely streaming consumed before capture). However the symptom proves shape is irrelevant: even the FIRST byte sequence of the body is not parseable as JSON, so V5-B {result:...} object vs V5-A [{result:...}] array distinction is moot.
network_observations.envelope_shape_observed: N/A — body never reaches JSON.parse success

console_state.new_error_type_vs_v5a: NO — same TRPCClientError class, same SyntaxError cause. V5-B did not change the symptom.
console_state.root_cause_diagnosis: The C++/server-side warning JSON payload contains malformed JSON (likely an unescaped " or stray byte inside the warning text body, possibly the Korean characters or the truncation routine cutting mid-property). tRPC envelope shape (array vs object) cannot be evaluated because JSON.parse fails before tRPC ever inspects the structure.

dom_scan.ui_state: Empty chat area — only Le Chat splash logo + 'Ask anything' input remained. No chat bubble, no warning bubble, no PII echo, no LLM response. Prompt input cleared after Enter (request fired) but no UI feedback.
dom_scan.auth_state: NOT signed in — 'Sign in / Sign up' buttons visible top-right. (V5-B engine endpoint /api/trpc/message.newChat does respond HTTP 200 even unauthenticated, indicating the warning interception fires server-side regardless of auth.)

recommended_next_step.rationale: V5-A → V5-B both produced identical TRPCClientError(SyntaxError). Further envelope-shape iterations (V5-C, V5-D) cannot succeed because the body never parses as JSON. The fix must be on the C++ payload generator: validate the emitted JSON with a real JSON encoder (do not concatenate strings), then re-test envelope shapes if needed.
recommended_next_step.suggested_action: Open Phase 6 (apf-warning-impl) on the C++ side: capture the raw response bytes via curl/tcpdump on the test bridge, JSON.parse them locally, and locate the exact byte offset where parsing fails. Likely culprits: (1) unescaped double-quote in the Korean warning string, (2) un-terminated property value, (3) BOM or stray control character, (4) truncation at MTU boundary cutting mid-token.

summary_200w: V5-B (tRPC v11 single-result object envelope) FAILED with the same TRPCClientError as V5-A. After hard-reload of chat.mistral.ai with DevTools Console+Network open, submitting the Korean RRN PII prompt produced: HTTP 200 on POST /api/trpc/message.newChat?batch=1 (39ms, ~0.2 kB), but the console immediately logged a tRPC mutation error whose .result.cause is a SyntaxError 'Expected , or } after property value'. The error CLASS is identical to V5-A — proving the array→object envelope swap is irrelevant; the response body is malformed JSON before tRPC inspects it. No warning bubble rendered, no LLM response, no PII decode. Recommendation: STOP iterating V5-C/D envelope variants and pivot to fixing the C++ payload generator to emit valid JSON. Likely causes are unescaped quotes, mid-token truncation, or stray control characters in the Korean warning string. All four screenshots captured to files/699/.
